title: Towards a quantum computer that learns from its errors
excerpt: Imagine a symphony orchestra performing a complex masterpiece. If the violins drifted out of tune every few measures, the ensemble would constantly have to stop and retune their instruments. Thankfully, this doesn't happen in an orchestra because the instruments reliably stay in tune. However, it is the current reality of operating a quantum computer.
content_html:
Imagine a symphony orchestra performing a complex masterpiece. If the violins drifted out of tune every few measures, the ensemble would constantly have to stop and retune their instruments. Thankfully, this doesn't happen in an orchestra because the instruments reliably stay in tune. However, it is the current reality of operating a quantum computer.

Since quantum computers are fundamentally analog machines that are sensitive to drift, maintaining reliable operation requires perpetually recalibrating their control parameters, i.e., the frequencies, amplitudes, and phases of the analog signals choreographing the qubits. Today, this requires fully terminating the entire quantum computation. This complete decoupling of computation and calibration represents a fundamental bottleneck for the future, as useful quantum algorithms must run continuously for days or even months.

To address this, in “Reinforcement learning control of quantum error correction”, published in Nature, we demonstrated a reinforcement learning (RL) framework in which an autonomous agent learns from quantum error detections to continuously steer thousands of control parameters, stabilizing the quantum system against drift during the computation. In short: we found a way to tune the instruments while the music plays.

Dealing with quantum errors

In a concert hall, a detuned instrument is immediately heard. The quantum realm offers no such luxury. As if the very act of listening ruined the performance, measuring the qubits collapses their quantum superposition states. To preserve the quantum information, we instead employ Quantum Error Correction (QEC), a technique that exploits redundancy to create “logical qubits” out of many physical qubits, and uses specialized parity checks on the physical qubits to digitize the analog noise into binary error detection events.

Unfortunately, these bits only tell us that an error occurred somewhere within a bounded spacetime region of the quantum circuit, not its exact location. It is like hearing a sour note without knowing exactly which musician played it. To pinpoint the likely error locations and calculate the necessary corrections, we rely on QEC decoders, such as the neural network decoder AlphaQubit (trained on real data) and algorithmic decoder Tesseract. If errors are sufficiently rare, these decoders can successfully restore the logical quantum information by analyzing the error detection data. However, decoders leave a crucial question unanswered: why did those errors happen in the first place?

Some errors result from the unavoidable interaction of a quantum system with its surrounding environment, leading to decoherence. This ruthless process destroys macroscopic quantum superpositions, effectively turning quantum computers into classical ones. This fundamental phenomenon is so pervasive that it causes our familiar classical reality to emerge from the underlying quantum laws of Nature. While these environmental errors can never be completely prevented, many others are manifestations of imprecise control calibration and hardware drift – flaws that remain within our power to mitigate.

Beyond traditional physics models

Traditionally, quantum calibration relied on physics models. Its techniques were refined through decades of quantum control research. However, across technological domains, human-crafted models inevitably hit a performance ceiling. Early computer vision stalled when relying on strict geometric rules. Traditional robotics still struggles with kinematic equations that fail to capture the messy reality of contact dynamics and friction. Similarly, the decades-old challenge of predicting protein folding remained largely intractable for traditional physical models until deep learning systems like AlphaFold achieved unprecedented accuracy. Across these fields, new breakthroughs occurred when the approach shifted toward learning directly from data.

Recently, AlphaQubit surpassed the accuracy of the most powerful algorithmic QEC decoders. Now quantum control faces the same ceiling. As quantum processors improve through progress in fabrication and hardware, their errors become dominated by complex phenomena that are challenging for traditional modeling and calibration. Can machine learning bring new advances?

Don’t just correct errors, learn from them!

Google Research has a rich history of pioneering RL to solve problems too complex for traditional programming. Unlike algorithms relying on explicit instructions, RL operates through experience. An autonomous agent tests different behaviors and learns directly from resulting errors to refine its strategy. Applying RL to achieve accurate, continuous quantum calibration felt almost inevitable. Since QEC already generates a steady stream of detection events, we simply granted this data a complementary role. In addition to decoding it and correcting the errors, we employ the detection events as an active learning signal. As computation progresses, the RL agent monitors this data and learns to dynamically steer the control parameters, counteracting drift and preventing new errors.

Our quantum control experiment

We validated such RL quantum control on our flagship Willow superconducting processor. By deliberately injecting artificial drift of control parameters, we showed that RL steering improved the logical stability of our error-correcting code 3.5-fold, prolonging the time during which the processor acts as a reliable “quantum memory” device.

Typically, tuning the processor to peak performance relied heavily on a “human-in-the-loop” approach, with scientists applying physical intuition to resolve edge cases that are difficult to automate. Yet, even after this exhaustive expert calibration, subsequent RL fine-tuning systematically suppressed the logical error rate by an additional 20%.

The synthesis of all our technologies in this experiment reduced the logical errors in quantum memories to a record low: fewer than one per thousand error correction cycles in the surface code, and one per hundred in the color code.

Does it scale?

A critical question from machine learning and quantum researchers is whether this RL approach can scale to large quantum computers of the future. To test this, we conducted numerical simulations with hundreds of qubits and tens of thousands of control parameters.

The simulations confirmed our expectation: the number of required RL training iterations (epochs) is independent of the system size, owing to the local sensitivity of the QEC detection events to errors. However, realizing the full potential of the RL framework requires tighter integration. By speeding up the communication cycle between the agent and the quantum processor, and employing more advanced machine learning methods, we hope to unlock significant additional improvements.

Our work thus enables a new paradigm: a quantum computer that learns from its errors and doesn’t stop computing.

Acknowledgements

We thank our co-authors for their contributions, including building and maintaining the hardware, software, cryogenics and electronics infrastructure. This work was made possible by the Google Quantum AI team at Google Research, in collaboration with teams from Google DeepMind.

blocks.html_en: Imagine a symphony orchestra performing a complex masterpiece. If the violins drifted out of tune every few measures, the ensemble would constantly have to stop and retune their instruments. Thankfully, this doesn't happen in an orchestra because the instruments reliably stay in tune. However, it is the current reality of operating a quantum computer.

sentences.en: Imagine a symphony orchestra performing a complex masterpiece.
sentences.zh: 想象一下一个交响乐团正在演奏一首复杂的杰作。

sentences.en: If the violins drifted out of tune every few measures, the ensemble would constantly have to stop and retune their instruments.
sentences.zh: 如果小提琴每隔几个小节就跑调，乐团就必须不断地停下来重新调整乐器。

sentences.en: Thankfully, this doesn't happen in an orchestra because the instruments reliably stay in tune.
sentences.zh: 值得庆幸的是，这种情况不会发生在管弦乐队中，因为乐器能够可靠地保持音调。

sentences.en: However, it is the current reality of operating a quantum computer.
sentences.zh: 然而，这就是操作量子计算机的当前现实。

blocks.html_en: Since quantum computers are fundamentally analog machines that are sensitive to drift, maintaining reliable operation requires perpetually recalibrating their control parameters, i.e., the frequencies, amplitudes, and phases of the analog signals choreographing the qubits. Today, this requires fully terminating the entire quantum computation. This complete decoupling of computation and calibration represents a fundamental bottleneck for the future, as useful quantum algorithms must run continuously for days or even months.

sentences.en: Since quantum computers are fundamentally analog machines that are sensitive to drift, maintaining reliable operation requires perpetually recalibrating their control parameters, i.e., the frequencies, amplitudes, and phases of the analog signals choreographing the qubits.
sentences.zh: 由于量子计算机本质上是对漂移敏感的模拟机器，因此保持可靠的运行需要不断地重新校准其控制参数，即编排量子位的模拟信号的频率、幅度和相位。

sentences.en: Today, this requires fully terminating the entire quantum computation.
sentences.zh: 今天，这需要完全终止整个量子计算。

sentences.en: This complete decoupling of computation and calibration represents a fundamental bottleneck for the future, as useful quantum algorithms must run continuously for days or even months.
sentences.zh: 这种计算和校准的完全解耦代表了未来的基本瓶颈，因为有用的量子算法必须连续运行数天甚至数月。

blocks.html_en: To address this, in “Reinforcement learning control of quantum error correction”, published in Nature, we demonstrated a reinforcement learning (RL) framework in which an autonomous agent learns from quantum error detections to continuously steer thousands of control parameters, stabilizing the quantum system against drift during the computation. In short: we found a way to tune the instruments while the music plays.

sentences.en: To address this, in “Reinforcement learning control of quantum error correction”, published in Nature, we demonstrated a reinforcement learning (RL) framework in which an autonomous agent learns from quantum error detections to continuously steer thousands of control parameters, stabilizing the quantum system against drift during the computation.
sentences.zh: 为了解决这个问题，在《自然》杂志上发表的“量子纠错的强化学习控制”中，我们演示了一种强化学习（RL）框架，其中自主代理从量子错误检测中学习，以连续引导数千个控制参数，从而稳定量子系统在计算过程中的漂移。

sentences.en: In short: we found a way to tune the instruments while the music plays.
sentences.zh: 简而言之：我们找到了一种在音乐播放时调整乐器的方法。

blocks.html_en: Dealing with quantum errors

sentences.en: Dealing with quantum errors
sentences.zh: 处理量子错误

blocks.html_en: In a concert hall, a detuned instrument is immediately heard. The quantum realm offers no such luxury. As if the very act of listening ruined the performance, measuring the qubits collapses their quantum superposition states. To preserve the quantum information, we instead employ Quantum Error Correction (QEC), a technique that exploits redundancy to create “logical qubits” out of many physical qubits, and uses specialized parity checks on the physical qubits to digitize the analog noise into binary error detection events.

sentences.en: In a concert hall, a detuned instrument is immediately heard.
sentences.zh: 在音乐厅中，立即可以听到失谐的乐器。

sentences.en: The quantum realm offers no such luxury.
sentences.zh: 量子领域不提供这样的奢侈。

sentences.en: As if the very act of listening ruined the performance, measuring the qubits collapses their quantum superposition states.
sentences.zh: 就好像聆听的行为本身就破坏了性能一样，测量量子位会破坏它们的量子叠加态。

sentences.en: To preserve the quantum information, we instead employ Quantum Error Correction (QEC), a technique that exploits redundancy to create “logical qubits” out of many physical qubits, and uses specialized parity checks on the physical qubits to digitize the analog noise into binary error detection events.
sentences.zh: 为了保留量子信息，我们采用量子纠错（QEC），这是一种利用冗余从许多物理量子位中创建“逻辑量子位”的技术，并对物理量子位使用专门的奇偶校验将模拟噪声数字化为二进制错误检测事件。

blocks.html_en: Unfortunately, these bits only tell us that an error occurred somewhere within a bounded spacetime region of the quantum circuit, not its exact location. It is like hearing a sour note without knowing exactly which musician played it. To pinpoint the likely error locations and calculate the necessary corrections, we rely on QEC decoders, such as the neural network decoder AlphaQubit (trained on real data) and algorithmic decoder Tesseract. If errors are sufficiently rare, these decoders can successfully restore the logical quantum information by analyzing the error detection data. However, decoders leave a crucial question unanswered: why did those errors happen in the first place?

sentences.en: Unfortunately, these bits only tell us that an error occurred somewhere within a bounded spacetime region of the quantum circuit, not its exact location.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，这些位只告诉我们错误发生在量子电路的有限时空区域内的某个地方，而不是其确切位置。

sentences.en: It is like hearing a sour note without knowing exactly which musician played it.
sentences.zh: 这就像听到一个酸涩的音符却不知道到底是哪位音乐家演奏的。

sentences.en: To pinpoint the likely error locations and calculate the necessary corrections, we rely on QEC decoders, such as the neural network decoder AlphaQubit (trained on real data) and algorithmic decoder Tesseract.
sentences.zh: 为了查明可能的错误位置并计算必要的校正，我们依靠 QEC 解码器，例如神经网络解码器 AlphaQubit（在真实数据上训练）和算法解码器 Tesseract。

sentences.en: If errors are sufficiently rare, these decoders can successfully restore the logical quantum information by analyzing the error detection data.
sentences.zh: 如果错误足够少，这些解码器可以通过分析错误检测数据成功地恢复逻辑量子信息。

sentences.en: However, decoders leave a crucial question unanswered: why did those errors happen in the first place?
sentences.zh: 然而，解码器留下了一个没有答案的关键问题：为什么这些错误首先发生？

blocks.html_en: Some errors result from the unavoidable interaction of a quantum system with its surrounding environment, leading to decoherence. This ruthless process destroys macroscopic quantum superpositions, effectively turning quantum computers into classical ones. This fundamental phenomenon is so pervasive that it causes our familiar classical reality to emerge from the underlying quantum laws of Nature. While these environmental errors can never be completely prevented, many others are manifestations of imprecise control calibration and hardware drift – flaws that remain within our power to mitigate.

sentences.en: Some errors result from the unavoidable interaction of a quantum system with its surrounding environment, leading to decoherence.
sentences.zh: 一些错误是由于量子系统与其周围环境不可避免的相互作用造成的，从而导致退相干。

sentences.en: This ruthless process destroys macroscopic quantum superpositions, effectively turning quantum computers into classical ones.
sentences.zh: 这种无情的过程破坏了宏观量子叠加态，有效地将量子计算机变成了经典计算机。

sentences.en: This fundamental phenomenon is so pervasive that it causes our familiar classical reality to emerge from the underlying quantum laws of Nature.
sentences.zh: 这种基本现象如此普遍，以至于我们熟悉的经典现实从自然的基本量子定律中显现出来。

sentences.en: While these environmental errors can never be completely prevented, many others are manifestations of imprecise control calibration and hardware drift – flaws that remain within our power to mitigate.
sentences.zh: 虽然这些环境错误永远无法完全避免，但许多其他错误是不精确的控制校准和硬件漂移的表现，这些缺陷仍然是我们有能力减轻的。

blocks.html_en: Beyond traditional physics models

sentences.en: Beyond traditional physics models
sentences.zh: 超越传统物理模型

blocks.html_en: Traditionally, quantum calibration relied on physics models. Its techniques were refined through decades of quantum control research. However, across technological domains, human-crafted models inevitably hit a performance ceiling. Early computer vision stalled when relying on strict geometric rules. Traditional robotics still struggles with kinematic equations that fail to capture the messy reality of contact dynamics and friction. Similarly, the decades-old challenge of predicting protein folding remained largely intractable for traditional physical models until deep learning systems like AlphaFold achieved unprecedented accuracy. Across these fields, new breakthroughs occurred when the approach shifted toward learning directly from data.

sentences.en: Traditionally, quantum calibration relied on physics models.
sentences.zh: 传统上，量子校准依赖于物理模型。

sentences.en: Its techniques were refined through decades of quantum control research.
sentences.zh: 其技术经过数十年的量子控制研究而得到完善。

sentences.en: However, across technological domains, human-crafted models inevitably hit a performance ceiling.
sentences.zh: 然而，在各个技术领域，人造模型不可避免地会遇到性能天花板。

sentences.en: Early computer vision stalled when relying on strict geometric rules.
sentences.zh: 早期的计算机视觉在依赖严格的几何规则时陷入停滞。

sentences.en: Traditional robotics still struggles with kinematic equations that fail to capture the messy reality of contact dynamics and friction.
sentences.zh: 传统的机器人技术仍然在与运动学方程作斗争，这些方程无法捕捉接触动力学和摩擦的混乱现实。

sentences.en: Similarly, the decades-old challenge of predicting protein folding remained largely intractable for traditional physical models until deep learning systems like AlphaFold achieved unprecedented accuracy.
sentences.zh: 同样，预测蛋白质折叠这一长达数十年的挑战对于传统物理模型来说仍然非常棘手，直到像 AlphaFold 这样的深度学习系统达到了前所未有的准确性。

sentences.en: Across these fields, new breakthroughs occurred when the approach shifted toward learning directly from data.
sentences.zh: 在这些领域中，当方法转向直接从数据中学习时，出现了新的突破。

blocks.html_en: Recently, AlphaQubit surpassed the accuracy of the most powerful algorithmic QEC decoders. Now quantum control faces the same ceiling. As quantum processors improve through progress in fabrication and hardware, their errors become dominated by complex phenomena that are challenging for traditional modeling and calibration. Can machine learning bring new advances?

sentences.en: Recently, AlphaQubit surpassed the accuracy of the most powerful algorithmic QEC decoders.
sentences.zh: 最近，AlphaQubit 超越了最强大的算法 QEC 解码器的准确性。

sentences.en: Now quantum control faces the same ceiling.
sentences.zh: 现在量子控制也面临着同样的天花板。

sentences.en: As quantum processors improve through progress in fabrication and hardware, their errors become dominated by complex phenomena that are challenging for traditional modeling and calibration.
sentences.zh: 随着量子处理器通过制造和硬件的进步而改进，它们的误差变得由复杂现象主导，这对传统建模和校准构成了挑战。

sentences.en: Can machine learning bring new advances?
sentences.zh: 机器学习能否带来新的进步？

blocks.html_en: Don’t just correct errors, learn from them!

sentences.en: Don’t just correct errors, learn from them!
sentences.zh: 不要只是纠正错误，还要从中学习！

blocks.html_en: Google Research has a rich history of pioneering RL to solve problems too complex for traditional programming. Unlike algorithms relying on explicit instructions, RL operates through experience. An autonomous agent tests different behaviors and learns directly from resulting errors to refine its strategy. Applying RL to achieve accurate, continuous quantum calibration felt almost inevitable. Since QEC already generates a steady stream of detection events, we simply granted this data a complementary role. In addition to decoding it and correcting the errors, we employ the detection events as an active learning signal. As computation progresses, the RL agent monitors this data and learns to dynamically steer the control parameters, counteracting drift and preventing new errors.

sentences.en: Google Research has a rich history of pioneering RL to solve problems too complex for traditional programming.
sentences.zh: 谷歌研究院在开创 RL 解决传统编程过于复杂的问题方面有着丰富的历史。

sentences.en: Unlike algorithms relying on explicit instructions, RL operates through experience.
sentences.zh: 与依赖显式指令的算法不同，强化学习通过经验进行操作。

sentences.en: An autonomous agent tests different behaviors and learns directly from resulting errors to refine its strategy.
sentences.zh: 自主代理测试不同的行为并直接从产生的错误中学习以完善其策略。

sentences.en: Applying RL to achieve accurate, continuous quantum calibration felt almost inevitable.
sentences.zh: 应用强化学习来实现精确、连续的量子校准几乎是不可避免的。

sentences.en: Since QEC already generates a steady stream of detection events, we simply granted this data a complementary role.
sentences.zh: 由于 QEC 已经生成了稳定的检测事件流，因此我们只是赋予这些数据补充作用。

sentences.en: In addition to decoding it and correcting the errors, we employ the detection events as an active learning signal.
sentences.zh: 除了解码和纠正错误之外，我们还将检测事件用作主动学习信号。

sentences.en: As computation progresses, the RL agent monitors this data and learns to dynamically steer the control parameters, counteracting drift and preventing new errors.
sentences.zh: 随着计算的进行，强化学习代理会监控这些数据并学习动态引导控制参数，抵消漂移并防止新的错误。

blocks.html_en: Our quantum control experiment

sentences.en: Our quantum control experiment
sentences.zh: 我们的量子控制实验

blocks.html_en: We validated such RL quantum control on our flagship Willow superconducting processor. By deliberately injecting artificial drift of control parameters, we showed that RL steering improved the logical stability of our error-correcting code 3.5-fold, prolonging the time during which the processor acts as a reliable “quantum memory” device.

sentences.en: We validated such RL quantum control on our flagship Willow superconducting processor.
sentences.zh: 我们在我们的旗舰 Willow 超导处理器上验证了这种 RL 量子控制。

sentences.en: By deliberately injecting artificial drift of control parameters, we showed that RL steering improved the logical stability of our error-correcting code 3.5-fold, prolonging the time during which the processor acts as a reliable “quantum memory” device.
sentences.zh: 通过故意注入控制参数的人工漂移，我们发现 RL 控制将纠错代码的逻辑稳定性提高了 3.5 倍，延长了处理器作为可靠“量子存储”设备的时间。

blocks.html_en: Typically, tuning the processor to peak performance relied heavily on a “human-in-the-loop” approach, with scientists applying physical intuition to resolve edge cases that are difficult to automate. Yet, even after this exhaustive expert calibration, subsequent RL fine-tuning systematically suppressed the logical error rate by an additional 20%.

sentences.en: Typically, tuning the processor to peak performance relied heavily on a “human-in-the-loop” approach, with scientists applying physical intuition to resolve edge cases that are difficult to automate.
sentences.zh: 通常，将处理器调整到峰值性能在很大程度上依赖于“人机交互”方法，科学家们应用物理直觉来解决难以自动化的边缘情况。

sentences.en: Yet, even after this exhaustive expert calibration, subsequent RL fine-tuning systematically suppressed the logical error rate by an additional 20%.
sentences.zh: 然而，即使经过如此详尽的专家校准，随后的 RL 微调系统性地将逻辑错误率又降低了 20%。

blocks.html_en: The synthesis of all our technologies in this experiment reduced the logical errors in quantum memories to a record low: fewer than one per thousand error correction cycles in the surface code, and one per hundred in the color code.

sentences.en: The synthesis of all our technologies in this experiment reduced the logical errors in quantum memories to a record low: fewer than one per thousand error correction cycles in the surface code, and one per hundred in the color code.
sentences.zh: 在这个实验中综合了我们所有的技术，将量子存储器中的逻辑错误降低到了历史最低水平：表面代码中的纠错周期少于千分之一，颜色代码中的纠错周期少于千分之一。

blocks.html_en: Does it scale?

sentences.en: Does it scale?
sentences.zh: 它能扩展吗？

blocks.html_en: A critical question from machine learning and quantum researchers is whether this RL approach can scale to large quantum computers of the future. To test this, we conducted numerical simulations with hundreds of qubits and tens of thousands of control parameters.

sentences.en: A critical question from machine learning and quantum researchers is whether this RL approach can scale to large quantum computers of the future.
sentences.zh: 机器学习和量子研究人员面临的一个关键问题是，这种强化学习方法是否可以扩展到未来的大型量子计算机。

sentences.en: To test this, we conducted numerical simulations with hundreds of qubits and tens of thousands of control parameters.
sentences.zh: 为了测试这一点，我们使用数百个量子位和数万个控制参数进行了数值模拟。

blocks.html_en: The simulations confirmed our expectation: the number of required RL training iterations (epochs) is independent of the system size, owing to the local sensitivity of the QEC detection events to errors. However, realizing the full potential of the RL framework requires tighter integration. By speeding up the communication cycle between the agent and the quantum processor, and employing more advanced machine learning methods, we hope to unlock significant additional improvements.

sentences.en: The simulations confirmed our expectation: the number of required RL training iterations (epochs) is independent of the system size, owing to the local sensitivity of the QEC detection events to errors.
sentences.zh: 模拟证实了我们的预期：由于 QEC 检测事件对错误的局部敏感性，所需的 RL 训练迭代（epoch）数量与系统规模无关。

sentences.en: However, realizing the full potential of the RL framework requires tighter integration.
sentences.zh: 然而，要充分发挥 RL 框架的潜力，需要更紧密的集成。

sentences.en: By speeding up the communication cycle between the agent and the quantum processor, and employing more advanced machine learning methods, we hope to unlock significant additional improvements.
sentences.zh: 通过加快代理和量子处理器之间的通信周期，并采用更先进的机器学习方法，我们希望能够实现重大的额外改进。

blocks.html_en: Our work thus enables a new paradigm: a quantum computer that learns from its errors and doesn’t stop computing.

sentences.en: Our work thus enables a new paradigm: a quantum computer that learns from its errors and doesn’t stop computing.
sentences.zh: 因此，我们的工作实现了一种新的范式：一台能够从错误中学习并且不会停止计算的量子计算机。

blocks.html_en: Acknowledgements

sentences.en: Acknowledgements
sentences.zh: 致谢

blocks.html_en: We thank our co-authors for their contributions, including building and maintaining the hardware, software, cryogenics and electronics infrastructure. This work was made possible by the Google Quantum AI team at Google Research, in collaboration with teams from Google DeepMind.

sentences.en: We thank our co-authors for their contributions, including building and maintaining the hardware, software, cryogenics and electronics infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 我们感谢我们的合著者的贡献，包括构建和维护硬件、软件、低温和电子基础设施。

sentences.en: This work was made possible by the Google Quantum AI team at Google Research, in collaboration with teams from Google DeepMind.
sentences.zh: 这项工作是由谷歌研究院的谷歌量子人工智能团队与谷歌 DeepMind 团队合作完成的。

content_html_zh:
想象一下一个交响乐团正在演奏一首复杂的杰作。 如果小提琴每隔几个小节就跑调，乐团就必须不断地停下来重新调整乐器。 值得庆幸的是，这种情况不会发生在管弦乐队中，因为乐器能够可靠地保持音调。 然而，这就是操作量子计算机的当前现实。

由于量子计算机本质上是对漂移敏感的模拟机器，因此保持可靠的运行需要不断地重新校准其控制参数，即编排量子位的模拟信号的频率、幅度和相位。 今天，这需要完全终止整个量子计算。 这种计算和校准的完全解耦代表了未来的基本瓶颈，因为有用的量子算法必须连续运行数天甚至数月。

为了解决这个问题，在《自然》杂志上发表的“量子纠错的强化学习控制”中，我们演示了一种强化学习（RL）框架，其中自主代理从量子错误检测中学习，以连续引导数千个控制参数，从而稳定量子系统在计算过程中的漂移。 简而言之：我们找到了一种在音乐播放时调整乐器的方法。

处理量子错误

在音乐厅中，立即可以听到失谐的乐器。 量子领域不提供这样的奢侈。 就好像聆听的行为本身就破坏了性能一样，测量量子位会破坏它们的量子叠加态。 为了保留量子信息，我们采用量子纠错（QEC），这是一种利用冗余从许多物理量子位中创建“逻辑量子位”的技术，并对物理量子位使用专门的奇偶校验将模拟噪声数字化为二进制错误检测事件。

不幸的是，这些位只告诉我们错误发生在量子电路的有限时空区域内的某个地方，而不是其确切位置。 这就像听到一个酸涩的音符却不知道到底是哪位音乐家演奏的。 为了查明可能的错误位置并计算必要的校正，我们依靠 QEC 解码器，例如神经网络解码器 AlphaQubit（在真实数据上训练）和算法解码器 Tesseract。 如果错误足够少，这些解码器可以通过分析错误检测数据成功地恢复逻辑量子信息。 然而，解码器留下了一个没有答案的关键问题：为什么这些错误首先发生？

一些错误是由于量子系统与其周围环境不可避免的相互作用造成的，从而导致退相干。 这种无情的过程破坏了宏观量子叠加态，有效地将量子计算机变成了经典计算机。 这种基本现象如此普遍，以至于我们熟悉的经典现实从自然的基本量子定律中显现出来。 虽然这些环境错误永远无法完全避免，但许多其他错误是不精确的控制校准和硬件漂移的表现，这些缺陷仍然是我们有能力减轻的。

超越传统物理模型

传统上，量子校准依赖于物理模型。 其技术经过数十年的量子控制研究而得到完善。 然而，在各个技术领域，人造模型不可避免地会遇到性能天花板。 早期的计算机视觉在依赖严格的几何规则时陷入停滞。 传统的机器人技术仍然在与运动学方程作斗争，这些方程无法捕捉接触动力学和摩擦的混乱现实。 同样，预测蛋白质折叠这一长达数十年的挑战对于传统物理模型来说仍然非常棘手，直到像 AlphaFold 这样的深度学习系统达到了前所未有的准确性。 在这些领域中，当方法转向直接从数据中学习时，出现了新的突破。

最近，AlphaQubit 超越了最强大的算法 QEC 解码器的准确性。 现在量子控制也面临着同样的天花板。 随着量子处理器通过制造和硬件的进步而改进，它们的误差变得由复杂现象主导，这对传统建模和校准构成了挑战。 机器学习能否带来新的进步？

不要只是纠正错误，还要从中学习！

谷歌研究院在开创 RL 解决传统编程过于复杂的问题方面有着丰富的历史。 与依赖显式指令的算法不同，强化学习通过经验进行操作。 自主代理测试不同的行为并直接从产生的错误中学习以完善其策略。 应用强化学习来实现精确、连续的量子校准几乎是不可避免的。 由于 QEC 已经生成了稳定的检测事件流，因此我们只是赋予这些数据补充作用。 除了解码和纠正错误之外，我们还将检测事件用作主动学习信号。 随着计算的进行，强化学习代理会监控这些数据并学习动态引导控制参数，抵消漂移并防止新的错误。

我们的量子控制实验

我们在我们的旗舰 Willow 超导处理器上验证了这种 RL 量子控制。 通过故意注入控制参数的人工漂移，我们发现 RL 控制将纠错代码的逻辑稳定性提高了 3.5 倍，延长了处理器作为可靠“量子存储”设备的时间。

通常，将处理器调整到峰值性能在很大程度上依赖于“人机交互”方法，科学家们应用物理直觉来解决难以自动化的边缘情况。 然而，即使经过如此详尽的专家校准，随后的 RL 微调系统性地将逻辑错误率又降低了 20%。

在这个实验中综合了我们所有的技术，将量子存储器中的逻辑错误降低到了历史最低水平：表面代码中的纠错周期少于千分之一，颜色代码中的纠错周期少于千分之一。

它能扩展吗？

机器学习和量子研究人员面临的一个关键问题是，这种强化学习方法是否可以扩展到未来的大型量子计算机。 为了测试这一点，我们使用数百个量子位和数万个控制参数进行了数值模拟。

模拟证实了我们的预期：由于 QEC 检测事件对错误的局部敏感性，所需的 RL 训练迭代（epoch）数量与系统规模无关。 然而，要充分发挥 RL 框架的潜力，需要更紧密的集成。 通过加快代理和量子处理器之间的通信周期，并采用更先进的机器学习方法，我们希望能够实现重大的额外改进。

因此，我们的工作实现了一种新的范式：一台能够从错误中学习并且不会停止计算的量子计算机。

致谢

我们感谢我们的合著者的贡献，包括构建和维护硬件、软件、低温和电子基础设施。 这项工作是由谷歌研究院的谷歌量子人工智能团队与谷歌 DeepMind 团队合作完成的。
